# Getting help from Skift

Stuck, curious, or want to discuss an idea? This document explains the fastest ways to get help and who to contact.

Whether you're exploring our public projects or using internal tools, the links and contacts below will point you in the right direction.

## Where to go

### 💬 GitHub Discussions — the community space

**Best for:** Broad conversations, brainstorming, demos, design feedback, or casual community chat.

[Join the conversation →](https://github.com/orgs/useskift/discussions)

### 🐛 GitHub Issues — for tracked problems

**Best for:** Concrete bugs, reproducible issues, feature requests, or anything that should be tracked and prioritized.

[Browse issue templates →](https://github.com/useskift/.github/issues/new/choose)

### 📧 Direct contact — email the right team

- **Customer & product support:** [hey@skift.work](mailto:hey@skift.work)
- **Community & collaboration:** [community@skift.work](mailto:community@skift.work)
- **Legal / CLA inquiries:** [legal@skift.work](mailto:legal@skift.work)
- **Security issues:** See our [Security Policy](SECURITY.md)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> We're people — responses may take a day or two, but we'll reply as soon as we can.

## Try these first

Before opening an issue or emailing us, these places often have the answer:

### 📚 Read the docs

- **Repository READMEs** — Setup steps, usage examples, and common troubleshooting notes live here.
- **Wiki pages** — For longer design notes and architecture documentation.
- **API docs** — If available, you'll usually find links in the repo README.
- **Examples & demos** — Check `examples/` or `demos/` folders for working samples.

### 🔎 Search smart

- **GitHub search** — Scan issues and discussions across the org; your question may already be answered.
- **Pull request notes** — PR descriptions and review threads often include helpful context and workarounds.

> [!TIP]
> Put error messages in quotes when searching — e.g. "TypeError: Cannot read property" — to find exact matches.

## How to get faster, more useful answers

### 📝 Make your question actionable

- **Give context:** What you're trying to do, steps you took, what happened vs. what you expected, and your environment (OS, versions, browser).
- **Include details:** Full error output, formatted code snippets, and screenshots or recordings if they help show the problem.
- **Stay focused:** One problem per issue, a clear title, and a concise description.

### 👍 Examples that help

#### Good examples — easy for us to act on

- "The docs say to run A but I get B. Here's my config and the exact error. Am I missing a step?"
- "I think there's a bug in the email validator — here's a minimal reproduction and steps to reproduce."

#### Unhelpful reports — and how to improve them

- "It doesn't work"  
   **Instead:** show what you tried, the expected result, and the actual output (include logs or screenshots).

- "Your software is broken"  
   **Instead:** explain the feature, provide reproduction steps, and why you think it's a bug.

- "This is urgent!!!" (with no details)  
   **Instead:** describe the impact, deadline, and any temporary workarounds so we can triage.

## Community support

### 🤝 Help others, help yourself

Even without being an expert, sharing what you learned helps others in similar situations.

If documentation is unclear, improve it or flag the problem — small fixes save time for everyone. When you publish a solution to an uncommon issue, add the steps you used so others can benefit.

**Remember when you were new to all of this?** Help welcome newcomers and point them to resources.

### 🌟 Want to be a regular helper?

Regular contributors often become go-to community helpers — it's a great way to give back. If you'd like a more formal mentoring or support role, email [community@skift.work](mailto:community@skift.work).

---

_If you read all of this and still need help, email [community@skift.work](mailto:community@skift.work)._
